parsed_review.summary: CP00-161 adds descriptor-only Master Data UI interaction workflow descriptors, fixtures, validators, tests, contract v0.6 bindings, H04/C04 packets, and CP00-162 handoff. No P0/P1 blockers were found. The reported P2 was fixed by adding security_display_rules parity validation. Two P3 items were fixed; the symbolic leak-check hardening item is explicitly deferred until real rendering/data packs.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-161 builds UI interaction descriptors only; package code does not execute API handlers, network calls, UI rendering, Hermes, or Claude.

findings.title: Contract ui_interaction_workflow.security_display_rules is not cross-checked against the registry constant
findings.evidence: The contract validator cross-checks ui_interaction_workflow.workflow_id, covered_slices, interaction_states, data_dependencies, fixture_ids, and evidence_descriptors between MASTER_DATA_UI_INTERACTION_WORKFLOW and contracts/master-data-contract.json, and validateMasterDataCp161UiInteractionWorkflow re-checks workflow_id and fixture_ids on the projection. Neither validates ui_interaction_workflow.security_display_rules (permission_badge_source, audit_hint_source, denied_copy_source, and especially prohibited_outputs). prohibited_outputs is the leak-prevention control list, so silent drift or removal in the contract JSON would go undetected. Runtime behavior is unaffected because createMasterDataUiInteractionWorkflowCatalog reads the registry constant directly, so this is a projection/consistency gap rather than a functional or security-behavior defect.
findings.recommended_action: Add requireEqual/requireIncludes checks asserting contract.ui_interaction_workflow.security_display_rules matches MASTER_DATA_UI_INTERACTION_WORKFLOW.security_display_rules (each of permission_badge_source, audit_hint_source, denied_copy_source, and every prohibited_outputs entry), mirroring the existing evidence_descriptors loop.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: contract validator and package validator now assert security_display_rules parity, including prohibited_outputs.

findings.title: C04 leak check matches category tokens rather than actual hidden-source values
findings.evidence: createMasterDataUiInteractionWorkflowCatalog computes prohibited_output_absent by substring-scanning serialized customer-facing descriptors for the literal labels raw_permission_rule, audit_internal_payload, internal_blocked_claim_refs, and hidden_source_values. This verifies absence of the category tokens, not the absence of actual prohibited content (e.g., real permission-rule text, audit payloads, or hidden_source_fields values such as raw_document_body). It is adequate for this synthetic, descriptor-only pack because no real data exists to leak, but the guard is symbolic.
findings.recommended_action: Defer for CP00-161. When a later pack renders real data, strengthen the leak check to scan for hidden_source_fields values and actual permission/audit payload content in addition to category tokens.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred: descriptor robustness hardening is tracked for a later pack and does not block this no-write reference closeout.

findings.title: Redundant default fixture_id transformation over an unreachable default path
findings.evidence: The default fixture_id builds a template that applies slice.replaceAll("_slice","").replaceAll("_","-") and then replaceAll("-","_") across the whole string, so the inner _→- and outer -→_ cancel out, making the transformation effectively a no-op. Every call site (catalog, model.test.js, validate-rp04-master-data-contract.mjs) passes an explicit fixture_id, so the default branch is never exercised. No behavioral impact.
findings.recommended_action: Simplify the default to `cp161_ui_fixture_${slice}_${scenario}` and drop the redundant replaceAll chain. Low priority.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: default fixture_id construction was simplified to cp161_ui_fixture_${slice}_${scenario}.

findings.title: One-directional requireIncludes checks allow undetected extra contract entries
findings.evidence: covered_slices, data_dependencies, and fixture_ids are validated with requireIncludes (registry subset of contract) but not the reverse, so stale or extra entries added only to the contract JSON would not be flagged. interaction_states and evidence_descriptors use per-key requireEqual which is stronger. Low risk because the contract is maintained alongside the registry.
findings.recommended_action: Optionally assert array length/equality for covered_slices, data_dependencies, and fixture_ids to catch contract-only additions.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: covered_slices, data_dependencies, and fixture_ids now use equality checks in addition to inclusion checks.
